basics.name: Anthony Nduka
basics.label: Catholic Priest | Data Scientist | Human-Centered AI Researcher
basics.summary: Catholic priest and data scientist with a strong background in AI, healthcare analytics, and ethical computing. Proven experience in interdisciplinary research, academic teaching, and technology solutions for underserved populations. Committed to advancing human-centered AI and socially impactful technologies.

work.name: Spiritan University, Nneochi
work.summary: Assisting in instruction and mentoring in programming, data analytics, and ethical technology courses.
highlights: Mentored undergraduate students on data projects and coursework.
Supported instructional delivery for Python and data science classes.
Provided grading and academic feedback to students.

work.name: Spiritan Healthcare Apostolate
work.summary: Leads a multi-diocesan health initiative serving elderly priests using predictive analytics and digitized care systems.
highlights: Developed predictive healthcare models for proactive elder care.
Digitized medical records to improve service coordination.
Over 155 elderly served across four Nigerian dioceses.

work.name: Spiritan Provincialate, Nigeria Southeast
work.summary: Oversees provincial records, digital transformation, and recognized for data excellence.
highlights: Best Data Manager Award by Spiritan Generalate, Rome (2022).
Digitized and streamlined provincial administrative data.
Authored biannual newsletter for 2000+ members.

work.name: Directorate of Social Communications, Spiritans
work.summary: Leads strategic communication for Catholic engagement and organizational transformation.
highlights: Trained 2500+ individuals in social communication practices.
Improved online visibility of 8 religious organizations.
Boosted digital Catholic awareness by 45%.

work.name: Spiritan Press Enterprise
work.summary: Oversaw operations, data analytics, and quality control across multiple publishing units.
highlights: Raised customer base by 40%.
Improved revenue by 25% using data-driven strategies.

education.thesisTitle: Development of INEC Registration and Voting System using Distributed Database

education.affiliation: Affiliate of Duquesne University of the Holy Spirit, Pennsylvania, USA

awards.title: Most Efficient Data Management Secretary
awards.summary: Awarded for excellence in provincial database administration.

awards.title: Best Paper Presenter
awards.awarder: National Association of Catholic Theology Students (NACATHS)
awards.summary: Awarded for outstanding paper presentation on Cyber Evangelization.

publications.name: Real-Time Edge Analytics for IoT Networks
publications.publisher: International Journal of Research and Innovation in Applied Science
publications.summary: Optimizing data processing and decision-making in smart cities using real-time analytics.

publications.name: Leveraging Big Data and IoT in Media Industry
publications.publisher: Under Review at Science and Technology Publishing
publications.summary: Explores the use of data and IoT to transform content creation, distribution, and audience engagement.

publications.name: Quantum Computing in Healthcare
publications.publisher: Under Review at International Journal of Innovative Science and Research Technology
publications.summary: Explores how quantum computing supports diagnostics, drug discovery, and personalized medicine.

projects.name: INEC Registration and Voting System
projects.summary: Developed a distributed database system integrated with biometric and voice recognition to improve Nigeria's voter registration and election credibility.
highlights: Improved electoral transparency using real-time data sync.
Enhanced access for physically challenged and relocated voters.

projects.name: Predicting Cardiovascular Diseases
projects.summary: Built a classification model to predict cardiovascular risk using medical data.
highlights: Achieved 86.96% accuracy using GridSearchCV tuning.
Applied model to ethical and real-world healthcare scenarios.

projects.name: Loan Data Analysis for Prosper Marketplace
projects.summary: Analyzed loan data to identify default risks and improve lending strategy.
highlights: Visualized patterns influencing loan performance.
Estimated 15% reduction in default rates.
